title: hippophae fluviatilis
intro: Hippophae fluviatilis, also known as Marsh Sea-buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, characterized by its thorny branches and small, yellow flowers. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, containing flavonoids, carotenoids, and ascorbic acid, which contribute to its health benefits. The berries of the Marsh Sea-buckthorn have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and skin conditions, due to their high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Hippophae fluviatilis include teas, juices, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and prevent diseases.
